## Overview
If you would like to visit Ireland for a short period (less than 3 months) you will find useful guidelines and details of requirements for Holiday and Visiting family / friend visa applications
                                                                        here.
                                                                    
Please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/saudi-arabia/)

## Visa Fees

[irl > sau > en > Table:Marriage or Medical > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in SAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge of SAR 226.20 per application over and above visa fees. (all applicable taxes included)
- Hub and spoke service charge 82.50 per application mandatory for applying in Jeddah and Dammam.82.50
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Visa fee exempted Nationalities (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2D9cMkZ7XuZJxFbh7bbToR/32f888e22249dbfe562ba9acc97fdaab/visa-fee-exempted-nationalities.pdf)
.

## Documents Required
Get the peace of mind you need on your travels, with insurance made simple for more click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/sau/en/irl/travel-insurance-non-schengen).
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation
                                                                        for the omission. 
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/7tZeCaP5ADN2i8bLpxGC0s/4aa85b1a06b31cba7c02552935749776/marriage-or-medical-checklist.pdf)  For Saudi Nationals
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6mlLUF57bOklg8b74xg9Q4/b9e1b3bb450d1c312f61fdcc35b6c01d/minor-visa-checklist.pdf) for Minors.
Click Here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/Ireland/SaudiArabia/english/pdf/ireland-vac-in-ksa-checklist.pdf) For Non Saudi Nationals 
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/OxoOcJPKssquTDipdgkXu/58879e87f7bc6096ed1d02af9eb5a697/obligation-to-return-home.pdf) for Obligation return to home

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
You can apply for a visit visa up to 6 months before your date of travel to Ireland. Tourist and Visit Visa Applications are usually processed within 1 month if all required documentation is in order. However, in some cases the procedure may take considerably longer. We advise you to apply for visa well in advance.
Kindly note that if you are applying from VFS in Jeddah/Al Khobar, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics

## Download Forms

                                                                        To apply online for a visa to travel to Ireland please visit the website of the Irish Naturalisation and  Immigration Service (http://www.inis.gov.ie/).
                                                                        You will find comprehensive information on the Irish visa application process on that site.

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.

- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).

visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you wish to come to Ireland to reside for longer than 3 months with a family member who is an Irish citizen or who is lawfully resident in Ireland you can apply for a long stay (join
                                                                        family) visa.
A comprehensive statement of national immigration policy on the area of family reunification is contained in the Policy Document on Non-EEA Family Reunification (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6itul9nbQ5QnMDhAiKQC1q/a64fe12899d1242fbaee6b1e565916d9/Family-Reunification-Policy-Document.pdf) published on 31 December 2013.
The policies outlined in the document apply to all decision making in relation to family reunification cases where ministerial discretion applies including long stay (join family) visa
                                                                        applications.
                                                                    
The policies do not apply to:
- applications pursuant to Directive 2504/38/EC on the rights of citizens of the Union and their family members to move and reside freely within the territory of Member States
- applications for family reunification by recognised refugees or persons granted subsidiary protection.
Before you apply for a long stay (join family) visa please read the Policy Document carefully to find out:
- who is eligible to be a sponsor for the purposes of an application for family reunification,
- who may be sponsored and the eligibility requirements,
- whether waiting periods apply before a person becomes eligible to sponsor,
- whether a sponsor must achieve a minimum level of earnings prior to being eligible to sponsor,
- what a person who is granted a join family visa may do in Ireland,
- how dependency is measured when this is adduced in support of an application for family reunification.
Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category.
Please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/saudi-arabia/)

Please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/saudi-arabia/)

## Visa Fees

[irl > sau > en > Table:Transit > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in SAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge of SAR 226.20 per application over and above visa fees. (all applicable taxes included)
- Hub and spoke service charge 82.50 per application mandatory for applying in Jeddah and Dammam.82.50
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Visa fee exempted Nationalities (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2D9cMkZ7XuZJxFbh7bbToR/32f888e22249dbfe562ba9acc97fdaab/visa-fee-exempted-nationalities.pdf)
.

## Documents Required
Get the peace of mind you need on your travels, with insurance made simple for more click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/sau/en/irl/travel-insurance-non-schengen).
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation
                                                                        for the omission. 
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
 Checklist (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/453eyRxazWrwTaZZZSkBoa/a22f786f5771032b4b894b2001534bdc/ireland-vac-in-ksa-checklist.pdf)
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/OxoOcJPKssquTDipdgkXu/58879e87f7bc6096ed1d02af9eb5a697/obligation-to-return-home.pdf) for Obligation return to home

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
Applicants may apply up to three months before their intended travel dates.
Processing times may increase during peak periods.
Kindly note that if you are applying from VFS in Jeddah/Al Khobar, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics

## Download Forms
To apply online for a visa to travel to Ireland please visit the website of the Irish Naturalisation and  Immigration Service (http://www.inis.gov.ie/).
                                                                        You will find comprehensive information on the Irish visa application process on that site.

## Overview
You can apply for this visa if you are working outside Ireland and want to come to Ireland to carry out activities relating to your job, such as to attend meetings, attend trade shows
                                                                        for promotional work, arrange deals or negotiate or sign trade agreements or contracts, or carry out fact finding missions. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls
                                                                        under this category.
Please follow this link in order to ascertain whether you require a visa to travel to Ireland: Click Here (https://www.dfa.ie/irish-embassy/saudi-arabia/)

## Visa Fees

[irl > sau > en > Table:Business > Visa Fees 1]
Please note:
- The applicable visa fee in SAR is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- Applicant shall be levied a service charge of SAR 226.20 per application over and above visa fees. (all applicable taxes included)
- Hub and spoke service charge 82.50 per application mandatory for applying in Jeddah and Dammam.82.50
- All fees are non-refundable.
- Visa fee exempted Nationalities (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/2D9cMkZ7XuZJxFbh7bbToR/32f888e22249dbfe562ba9acc97fdaab/visa-fee-exempted-nationalities.pdf)
.

## Documents Required
Get the peace of mind you need on your travels, with insurance made simple for more click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/sau/en/irl/travel-insurance-non-schengen).
This document is one of the forms you need to submit with your application. If any of the required documents listed in the checklist are missing. Please provide a written explanation
                                                                        for the omission. 
Gather your documents in order of the checklist and tick each item.
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/3vsNn3bAMAm2yl2kLYzVqm/4339879a7d78e44354264cb27d3b6176/business-checklist.pdf)  For Saudi Nationals
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/6mlLUF57bOklg8b74xg9Q4/b9e1b3bb450d1c312f61fdcc35b6c01d/minor-visa-checklist.pdf) for Minors.
Click Here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/Ireland/SaudiArabia/english/pdf/ireland-vac-in-ksa-checklist.pdf) For Non Saudi Nationals 
Click Here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/OxoOcJPKssquTDipdgkXu/58879e87f7bc6096ed1d02af9eb5a697/obligation-to-return-home.pdf) for Obligation return to home

## Photo Specifications
General
- The photograph for each visa applicant must be an un-mounted, full face photo, color photo as described below
- The photograph must have been taken within the last six months.
- Two photographs must be submitted.
Photo Size
The photo for each visa applicant submitted must measure:
- 35 mm x 45 mm with the head centered in the frame.
- The head (measured from the top of the hair to the bottom of the chin) should measure at least 30 mm
Photo Appearance
- The photograph must be a full-face front view in which the visa applicant is facing the camera directly.
- The face should cover about 70-80 percent of the photo area.
- The photograph MUST be in color and have only a white or light background. Photos with dark, busy, or patterned backgrounds will not be accepted
- In general, the applicant's head, including both face and hair, should be shown from the crown of the head to the tip of the chin on top and bottom and from hairline side-to-side.
                                                                            It is preferable that ears be exposed
- Sunglasses or other wear, which detracts from the face, are not acceptable unless required for medical reasons (an eye patch, for example).
- A photograph depicting a person wearing a traditional facemask or veil that does not permit adequate identification is not acceptable.
Note: Please follow these instructions carefully. If photographs presented do not meet these requirements your application will be considered incomplete.

## Processing Time
You can apply for a business visa up to 6 months before your date of travel to Ireland. However, in some cases the procedure may take considerably longer. We advise you to apply for visa
                                                                        well in advance.
Kindly note that if you are applying from VFS in Jeddah/Al Khobar, it will take additional 48 hours due to logistics

## Download Forms
 To apply online for a visa to travel to Ireland please visit the website of the Irish Naturalisation and  Immigration Service (http://www.inis.gov.ie/).
                                                                        You will find comprehensive information on the Irish visa application process on that site.
